summary: Verified two P0 fixes: (1) All four card-click flows open the correct detail modal (Dashboard recipe-mini, Dashboard story-mini, Recipes page card, Stories page card). (2) Backend _generate_recipe_image (Gemini Nano Banana) helper returns a realistic JPEG data URL (>50KB). Heart button on recipe cards correctly uses stopPropagation and does not open the modal, while toggling the liked state.

minor.issue: Skipped in automated test — synthetic short WAV likely rejected by Sarvam ASR. Direct helper _generate_recipe_image was validated instead and produced a ~50KB+ JPEG.

Does NOT accept 'liked' field (only cover/title/region/serves/time/tags/ingredients/steps). Frontend uses POST /api/recipes/{id}/like instead which works — but the PATCH allow-list mismatch is worth noting for future consumers.

issues: Recipe card is an outer <button>, and the heart/share controls inside are <span role="button"> — semantically invalid to nest interactive controls inside a button. Consider changing outer element to a div with role="button" tabIndex=0 (or keep button but remove nested role=button spans).
No data-testid on the inner heart/share span buttons — testability gap. Add data-testid="recipe-like-{id}" and "recipe-share-{id}" for reliable automation.

action_items: Add data-testid to per-card like and share buttons in RecipesPage.jsx (e.g., recipe-like-{id}, recipe-share-{id}).
Consider replacing the outer <button> for recipe cards with a div[role=button] to avoid nested interactive elements.
Optional: expand PATCH /api/recipes allow-list to include 'liked' for API consistency, OR document that liking must use POST /recipes/{id}/like.
critical_code_review_comments: Nested interactive elements on Recipes page cards: <button> containing <span role=button>. Not fatal (works in Chromium) but violates HTML nesting rules and hurts accessibility.
_generate_recipe_image already has SVG-emoji fallback on Gemini failure — good resilience. Verified live: returned base64 JPEG ~50KB+ for 'Aloo Paratha'.
server.py is 1000+ lines — consider splitting into routers (recipes, stories, families, smart_record, media) and ai_helpers module.
@app.on_event deprecation warning — migrate to lifespan handlers.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 test story via POST /api/stories with title 'Test Story ABC' to exercise story-card / story-mini flows (no DELETE /stories endpoint exists to remove it — it will remain in the demo dataset).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/. Use `pytest backend/tests/ -n 0` (pytest.ini forces xdist; -n 0 to serialize). test_recipe_image_helper.py directly invokes async _generate_recipe_image via asyncio.run — takes ~8s (real Gemini call). Frontend: all four card-click flows verified with Playwright; test story 'Test Story ABC' has been left in DB because no delete endpoint exists. Heart button on recipe card is a <span role=button> with no data-testid — I used a positional selector.
rca of the issue: Both original P0s appear resolved. (1) Card openability was a missing onClick handler after a mobile refactor — main agent fixed by making cards buttons wired to setOpenRecipe/setOpenStory and mounting new RecipeDetailModal/StoryDetailModal components. Verified all four entry points. (2) Realistic covers regressed to emoji-only earlier — main agent restored Gemini Nano Banana (gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview) via emergentintegrations with a photorealistic food-photography prompt and SVG fallback. Direct helper call returned a valid JPEG data URL >50KB; new smart-record recipes will get realistic covers while story/festival remain emoji-SVG per user preference.
